description_long: This is a classic pipe wrench featuring a robust, dark metallic head with an adjustable jaw mechanism and a long, cylindrical wooden handle. The head of the wrench is composed of heavy metal, showing a toothed, adjustable jaw for gripping pipes or fittings. The adjustment mechanism is visible as a knurled, threaded section near the head, allowing the jaw width to be changed. The handle is made of wood, displaying a worn, textured surface with visible grain and some discoloration from use. The metal parts show signs of oxidation and wear, particularly around the jaw and adjustment area, indicating frequent handling. Overall, the tool has a utilitarian, aged appearance typical of hand tools found in urban garages or workshops.
description: A vintage pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable jaw and a worn wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use and age. The tool features a toothed jaw and a threaded adjustment mechanism.
description_view_0: The wrench is viewed head-on, highlighting the circular jaw opening and the adjustment mechanism, with the handle receding into the background.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the full length of the wrench, the detailed shape of the jaw, and the textured wooden handle.
description_view_2: The handle is prominent in this rear view, showing its rounded end and wood grain, while the jaw is less visible.
description_view_3: Another side profile, opposite to view 1, displaying the jaw's structure and the handle's length, with visible wear on the wood.
attribute_handle_grip_pattern: The handle grip area is smooth wood with visible grain and discoloration, but no distinct grip pattern or knurling.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Visible wear and discoloration on the wooden handle, with some oxidation and minor rust marks on the metal jaw and adjustment mechanism.
